prompt: My `UserService` class has 12 public methods — password reset, user search, account activation, deletion, profile updates, role assignment, audit-log access, etc. Different consumers use different subsets: the admin panel uses 4 of them, the password-reset email handler uses 2, the public profile page uses 3. How should I structure the interface(s) so this follows ISP?

assertions.text: Recommends role interfaces (multiple small per-client interfaces), NOT a single header interface
Gives concrete role-interface examples (e.g., PasswordResetter, UserSearcher, UserActivator) defined from the client's perspective
States that UserServiceImpl implements all the role interfaces
Each consumer depends only on the role interface it actually uses
Names or describes the header-interface anti-pattern (a single mirror interface of the class is useful for mocking but useless for ISP)
